insights.claim: ZIPs in the most-deprived tenth (by ADI) average 2.7 points higher than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted decile means over Area Deprivation Index.

insights.claim: Across ZIPs, coronary heart disease is most associated with median household income (Spearman ρ=-0.592, n=29,492).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation, ecological (ZIP-level); not causal.
Maximum ZIP value (ties broken by population).

insights.claim: About 8.3 million people live in ZIPs above the high-burden threshold (9.4%).
insights.method_note: Sum of population in ZIPs at or above the 90th percentile (9.4%).
